exercises.title: Simulate File Write with StringIO
exercises.description: Practice the patterns used when writing to files, but using io.StringIO as an in-memory buffer. Write lines, get the full content, count bytes written.
exercises.starterCode:
import io

# In a real program this would be open('output.txt', 'w')
# In Pyodide we simulate it with StringIO
buffer = io.StringIO()

lines = [
    "Python is a versatile language.",
    "It is used for web development.",
    "Python supports data science.",
]

# TODO: Write each line to buffer followed by '\n'
# Use buffer.write(line + '\n') for each

# TODO: Get the full content from the buffer using buffer.getvalue()

# TODO: Print the total number of characters written
# Format: "Characters written: 84"

# TODO: Print the content line by line (strip trailing newline from each)
exercises.expectedOutput:
Characters written: 84
Python is a versatile language.
It is used for web development.
Python supports data science.

exercises.title: Simulate Append Mode with a Log List
exercises.description: Simulate write vs append modes using lists. Build a log where 'write mode' clears existing content and 'append mode' adds to it. Format each log entry and print the final log.
exercises.starterCode:
import io

# Simulate a log file that starts empty
log_buffer = io.StringIO()

# First 'write' - simulates open('log.txt', 'w')
# This overwrites any previous content
log_buffer = io.StringIO()  # New StringIO = fresh file
log_buffer.write("[INFO] Application started\n")
log_buffer.write("[INFO] Loading configuration\n")

# TODO: Simulate 'append' mode by keeping the buffer and adding more lines
# Write these two lines to log_buffer:
# "[WARNING] Config file not found, using defaults"
# "[INFO] Server started on port 8080"

# TODO: Get all content and split into lines

# TODO: Print total number of log entries
# Format: "Log entries: 4"

# TODO: Print each log entry
exercises.expectedOutput:
Log entries: 4
[INFO] Application started
[INFO] Loading configuration
[WARNING] Config file not found, using defaults
[INFO] Server started on port 8080
hints: log_buffer.write("[WARNING] Config file not found, using defaults\n")

exercises.title: Format and Write a Structured Report
exercises.description: Write a formatted student report to an in-memory buffer. Use consistent formatting with headers, separators, and per-student sections. Then extract and print a summary from the report.
exercises.starterCode:
import io

students = [
    {"name": "Alice", "gpa": 3.9, "courses": 5},
    {"name": "Bob", "gpa": 3.5, "courses": 4},
    {"name": "Carol", "gpa": 3.7, "courses": 6},
]

buffer = io.StringIO()

# TODO: Write a header to buffer:
# "=" * 40 + "\n"
# "STUDENT REPORT\n"
# "=" * 40 + "\n"

# TODO: For each student, write:
# "Name: Alice\n"
# "GPA:  3.90\n"
# "Courses: 5\n"
# "-" * 20 + "\n"

# TODO: Get content from buffer and print it

# TODO: Also compute and print the average GPA
# Format: "Average GPA: 3.70"
hints: print(buffer.getvalue(), end='') to avoid extra newline
avg = sum(s['gpa'] for s in students) / len(students)

warmup.title: Quick Recall: StringIO Basics
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace the StringIO write and getvalue operations.
warmup.starterCode:
import io

buf = io.StringIO()
buf.write("Hello")
buf.write(", ")
buf.write("World!")
content = buf.getvalue()
print(len(content))
print(content)
